behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real model output quality (speaker diarization/label preservation). MOCK-HIDE: a stub transcript with perfect labels would always pass and hide real diarization failures. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (System->Agentic): a real HTTP STT call with deterministic mocked response passes, but real STT on overlapping voices drops speaker labels, so attribution fails. REJECTION-GATE (Workflow rejected): behavior text targets one model-stage property ("speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels"), not a required multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run STT against real meeting audio with multiple speakers and interruptions; assert transcript includes stable speaker labels per utterance and labels are usable by attribution logic (no unknown/missing speaker on attributed statements).

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is deterministic parsing/normalization logic on transcript text, with no required I/O or model judgment. MOCK-MISS check: this behavior can fail within one parser function/object boundary. Escalations rejected: no behavior text requiring real OS/network/subprocess, real model output, or multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Pure parser test: feed transcript snippets with explicit assignments (including edge punctuation/name formats) and assert emitted structure contains correct assignee and task text pairs.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on LLM content decisions (coverage of assigned attendees). Deterministic wrappers can pass while real model omits one attendee. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (System->Agentic): a system test with mocked summary JSON including all attendees passes, but real LLM summary omits action item for one assigned attendee. REJECTION-GATE (Workflow rejected): behavior text focuses on one output-quality criterion of the live summary, not explicitly on correctness across multiple model decision stages.
behaviors.test_description: Use real transcript(s) with known per-attendee assignments; call real summarizer and assert each assigned attendee appears in action items at least once.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at module handoff: identified-decision outputs must map into schema assembler fields. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Unit->Integration): router unit test with canonical decision object passes, but when wired to real identifier output shape, mapping mismatch sends items to narrative fallback. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess required, no dependence on model output quality, and no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Wire decision-identifier module output into summary schema builder in-process (fixtures/in-memory); assert identified decisions land in `decisions` array and not narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This is LLM output-quality and placement behavior (what is selected as key decision and where it is placed). MOCK-HIDE: stubbed model output guarantees placement and hides real misses/misplacement. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (System->Agentic): real HTTP path with mocked deterministic summary passes, but real LLM puts a key decision in narrative text only. REJECTION-GATE (Workflow rejected): behavior text asks for final placement outcome, not explicitly a required multi-agentic-stage sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real summarization on transcripts with known key decisions; assert those decisions appear in structured `decisions` section and are not only present in narrative fields.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Behavior requires multiple agentic steps in sequence (segment-level transcribe/summarize and merged final coherence). MULTI-STEP applies directly. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Agentic->Workflow): single-segment real-model test passes, but in multi-segment run the merge produces inconsistent/duplicated items due cross-segment handoff and sequential context drift. REJECTION-GATE (higher level none): Workflow is highest defined level.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real >60-minute recording; execute full segmented processing with real STT/LLM calls; assert segment ordering, complete merge, and coherent unified structured summary (no dropped/duplicated segment-level decisions/actions).

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is explicit end-to-end pipeline behavior with real recording, real STT and real LLM stages, and sequential handoffs. MULTI-STEP + LLM-DEP both apply. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Agentic->Workflow): a single real summarization call succeeds, but full pipeline fails at stage transition (upload->transcribe->summarize->delivery) requiring manual retry. REJECTION-GATE (higher level none): Workflow is highest defined level.
behaviors.test_description: Run production-like E2E with real meeting audio and real external services from upload through final delivery endpoint/store; assert autonomous completion and valid structured payload containing expected top-level sections.
